title: Glochidion reinwardtii
taxonomy: Glochidion reinwardtii is classified within the family Phyllanthaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the 'leafy greens' or 'maiden's hair'. This family comprises approximately 1,000 species, mostly found in the tropical regions of the world. Glochidion reinwardtii, specifically, is native to Southeast Asia and belongs to the genus Glochidion, which contains around 150 species. The classification of Glochidion reinwardtii as a species is further divided into various subspecies and varieties based on its distinct characteristics. As a member of the Phyllanthaceae family, Glochidion reinwardtii exhibits unique leaf structures and reproductive patterns.
